name: nflows
documentation: # nflows `nflows` is a comprehensive collection of [normalizing flows](https://arxiv.org/abs/1912.02762) using [PyTorch](https://pytorch.org). ## Installation To install from PyPI: ``` pip install nflows ``` ## Usage To define a flow: ```python from nflows import transforms, distributions, flows # Define an invertible transformation. transform = transforms.CompositeTransform([ transforms.MaskedAffineAutoregressiveTransform(features=2, hidden_features=4), transforms.RandomPermutation(features=2) ]) # Define a base distribution. base_distribution = distributions.StandardNormal(shape=[2]) # Combine into a flow. flow = flows.Flow(transform=transform, distribution=base_distribution) ``` To evaluate log probabilities of inputs: ```python log_prob = flow.log_prob(inputs) ``` To sample from the flow: ```python samples = flow.sample(num_samples) ``` Additional examples of the workflow are provided in [examples folder](examples/). ## Development You can install all the dependencies using the `environment.yml` file to create a conda environment: ``` conda env create -f environment.yml ``` Alternatively, you can install via `setup.py` (the `dev` flag installs development and testing dependencies): ``` pip install -e ".[dev]" ``` ## Citing nflows To cite the package: ```bibtex @software{nflows, author = {Conor Durkan and Artur Bekasov and Iain Murray and George Papamakarios}, title = {{nflows}: normalizing flows in {PyTorch}}, month = nov, year = 2020, publisher = {Zenodo}, version = {v0.14}, doi = {10.5281/zenodo.4296287}, url = {https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.4296287} } ``` The version number is intended to be the one from `nflows/version.py`. The year/month correspond to the date of the release. BibTeX entries for other versions could be found on [Zenodo](https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.4296286). If you're using spline-based flows in particular, consider citing the _Neural Spline Flows_ paper: [[bibtex]](https://gpapamak.github.io/bibtex/neural_spline_flows.bib). ## References `nflows` is derived from [bayesiains/nsf](https://github.com/bayesiains/nsf) originally published with > C. Durkan, A. Bekasov, I. Murray, G. Papamakarios, _Neural Spline Flows_, NeurIPS 2019. > [[arXiv]](https://arxiv.org/abs/1906.04032) [[bibtex]](https://gpapamak.github.io/bibtex/neural_spline_flows.bib) `nflows` has been used in > Conor Durkan, Iain Murray, George Papamakarios, _On Contrastive Learning for Likelihood-free Inference_, ICML 2020. > [[arXiv]](https://arxiv.org/abs/2002.03712). > Artur Bekasov, Iain Murray, _Ordering Dimensions with Nested Dropout Normalizing Flows_. > [[arXiv]](https://arxiv.org/abs/2006.08777). > Tim Dockhorn, James A. Ritchie, Yaoliang Yu, Iain Murray, _Density Deconvolution with Normalizing Flows_. > [[arXiv]](https://arxiv.org/abs/2006.09396). `nflows` is used by the conditional density estimation package [pyknos](https://github.com/mackelab/pyknos), and in turn the likelihood-free inference framework [sbi](https://github.com/mackelab/sbi).

modules.name: nflows.flows.autoregressive
documentation: Implementations of autoregressive flows.

classes.name: nflows.flows.autoregressive.MaskedAutoregressiveFlow
documentation:
An autoregressive flow that uses affine transforms with masking.

Reference:
> G. Papamakarios et al., Masked Autoregressive Flow for Density Estimation,
> Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems, 2017.

modules.name: nflows.flows.base
documentation: Basic definitions for the flows module.

classes.name: nflows.flows.base.Flow

methods.name: nflows.flows.base.Flow.__init__
documentation:
Constructor.

Args:
    transform: A `Transform` object, it transforms data into noise.
    distribution: A `Distribution` object, the base distribution of the flow that
        generates the noise.
    embedding_net: A `nn.Module` which has trainable parameters to encode the
        context (condition). It is trained jointly with the flow.

methods.name: nflows.flows.base.Flow.sample_and_log_prob
documentation:
Generates samples from the flow, together with their log probabilities.

For flows, this is more efficient that calling `sample` and `log_prob` separately.

methods.name: nflows.flows.base.Flow.transform_to_noise
documentation:
Transforms given data into noise. Useful for goodness-of-fit checking.

Args:
    inputs: A `Tensor` of shape [batch_size, ...], the data to be transformed.
    context: A `Tensor` of shape [batch_size, ...] or None, optional context associated
        with the data.

Returns:
    A `Tensor` of shape [batch_size, ...], the noise.
Base class for all flow objects.

modules.name: nflows.flows.realnvp
documentation: Implementations of Real NVP.

classes.name: nflows.flows.realnvp.SimpleRealNVP
documentation:
An simplified version of Real NVP for 1-dim inputs.

This implementation uses 1-dim checkerboard masking but doesn't use multi-scaling.

Reference:
> L. Dinh et al., Density estimation using Real NVP, ICLR 2017.

methods.name: nflows.distributions.uniform.BoxUniform.__init__
documentation:
Multidimensionqal uniform distribution defined on a box.

A `Uniform` distribution initialized with e.g. a parameter vector low or high of length 3 will result in a /batch/ dimension of length 3. A log_prob evaluation will then output three numbers, one for each of the independent Uniforms in the batch. Instead, a `BoxUniform` initialized in the same way has three /event/ dimensions, and returns a scalar log_prob corresponding to whether the evaluated point is in the box defined by low and high or outside. 

Refer to torch.distributions.Uniform and torch.distributions.Independent for further documentation.

Args:
    low (Tensor or float): lower range (inclusive).
    high (Tensor or float): upper range (exclusive).
    reinterpreted_batch_ndims (int): the number of batch dims to
                                     reinterpret as event dims.

modules.name: nflows.distributions.base
documentation: Basic definitions for the distributions module.

classes.name: nflows.distributions.base.NoMeanException
documentation: Exception to be thrown when a mean function doesn't exist.

classes.name: nflows.distributions.base.Distribution

methods.name: nflows.distributions.base.Distribution.log_prob
documentation:
Calculate log probability under the distribution.

Args:
    inputs: Tensor, input variables.
    context: Tensor or None, conditioning variables. If a Tensor, it must have the same
        number or rows as the inputs. If None, the context is ignored.

Returns:
    A Tensor of shape [input_size], the log probability of the inputs given the context.

methods.name: nflows.distributions.base.Distribution.sample
documentation:
Generates samples from the distribution. Samples can be generated in batches.

Args:
    num_samples: int, number of samples to generate.
    context: Tensor or None, conditioning variables. If None, the context is ignored.
    batch_size: int or None, number of samples per batch. If None, all samples are generated
        in one batch.

Returns:
    A Tensor containing the samples, with shape [num_samples, ...] if context is None, or
    [context_size, num_samples, ...] if context is given.

methods.name: nflows.distributions.base.Distribution.sample_and_log_prob
documentation:
Generates samples from the distribution together with their log probability.

Args:
    num_samples: int, number of samples to generate.
    context: Tensor or None, conditioning variables. If None, the context is ignored.

Returns:
    A tuple of:
        * A Tensor containing the samples, with shape [num_samples, ...] if context is None,
          or [context_size, num_samples, ...] if context is given.
        * A Tensor containing the log probabilities of the samples, with shape
          [num_samples, ...] if context is None, or [context_size, num_samples, ...] if
          context is given.
Base class for all distribution objects.

modules.name: nflows.transforms.permutations
documentation: Implementations of permutation-like transforms.

classes.name: nflows.transforms.permutations.Permutation
documentation: Permutes inputs on a given dimension using a given permutation.

classes.name: nflows.transforms.permutations.RandomPermutation
documentation: Permutes using a random, but fixed, permutation. Only works with 1D inputs.

classes.name: nflows.transforms.permutations.ReversePermutation
documentation: Reverses the elements of the input. Only works with 1D inputs.

classes.name: nflows.transforms.reshape.SqueezeTransform
documentation:
A transformation defined for image data that trades spatial dimensions for channel
dimensions, i.e. "squeezes" the inputs along the channel dimensions.

Implementation adapted from https://github.com/pclucas14/pytorch-glow and
https://github.com/chaiyujin/glow-pytorch.

Reference:
> L. Dinh et al., Density estimation using Real NVP, ICLR 2017.

Cost:

where:
    D = num of features
    N = num of inputs

modules.name: nflows.transforms.made
documentation: Implementation of MADE.

documentation: Returns the degrees an input to MADE should have.

classes.name: nflows.transforms.made.MaskedLinear
documentation: A linear module with a masked weight matrix.

classes.name: nflows.transforms.made.MaskedFeedforwardBlock
documentation:
A feedforward block based on a masked linear module.

NOTE: In this implementation, the number of output features is taken to be equal to
the number of input features.

classes.name: nflows.transforms.made.MaskedResidualBlock
documentation: A residual block containing masked linear modules.

classes.name: nflows.transforms.made.MADE
documentation:
Implementation of MADE.

It can use either feedforward blocks or residual blocks (default is residual).
Optionally, it can use batch norm or dropout within blocks (default is no).

documentation:
Constructor.

Args:
    transforms: an iterable of `Transform` objects.
Composes several transforms into one, in the order they are given.

classes.name: nflows.transforms.base.MultiscaleCompositeTransform

methods.name: nflows.transforms.base.MultiscaleCompositeTransform.__init__
documentation:
Constructor.

Args:
    num_transforms: int, total number of transforms to be added.
    split_dim: dimension along which to split.

methods.name: nflows.transforms.base.MultiscaleCompositeTransform.add_transform
documentation:
Add a transform. Must be called exactly `num_transforms` times.

Parameters:
    transform: the `Transform` object to be added.
    transform_output_shape: tuple, shape of transform's outputs, excl. the first batch
        dimension.

Returns:
    Input shape for the next transform, or None if adding the last transform.

A multiscale composite transform as described in the RealNVP paper.

Splits the outputs along the given dimension after every transform, outputs one half, and
passes the other half to further transforms. No splitting is done before the last transform.

Note: Inputs could be of arbitrary shape, but outputs will always be flattened.

Reference:
> L. Dinh et al., Density estimation using Real NVP, ICLR 2017.

Constructor.

Args:

        * If `mask[i] > 0`, `input[i]` will be transformed.
        * If `mask[i] <= 0`, `input[i]` will be passed unchanged.

documentation: Inverse of the coupling transform.

A base class for coupling layers. Supports 2D inputs (NxD), as well as 4D inputs for
images (NxCxHxW). For images the splitting is done on the channel dimension, using the
provided 1D mask.

classes.name: nflows.transforms.coupling.AffineCouplingTransform
documentation:
An affine coupling layer that scales and shifts part of the variables.

Reference:
> L. Dinh et al., Density estimation using Real NVP, ICLR 2017.

classes.name: nflows.transforms.coupling.AdditiveCouplingTransform
documentation:
An additive coupling layer, i.e. an affine coupling layer without scaling.

Reference:
> L. Dinh et al., NICE:  Non-linear  Independent  Components  Estimation,

modules.name: nflows.transforms.autoregressive
documentation: Implementations of autoregressive transforms.

classes.name: nflows.transforms.autoregressive.AutoregressiveTransform
documentation:
Transforms each input variable with an invertible elementwise transformation.

The parameters of each invertible elementwise transformation can be functions of previous input
variables, but they must not depend on the current or any following input variables.

NOTE: Calculating the inverse transform is D times slower than calculating the
forward transform, where D is the dimensionality of the input to the transform.

weight = O(1)

methods.name: nflows.transforms.linear.NaiveLinear.weight_inverse

where:

methods.name: nflows.transforms.linear.NaiveLinear.weight_inverse_and_logabsdet

    logabsdet = O(D)
where:
    D = num of features

methods.name: nflows.transforms.linear.NaiveLinear.logabsdet
documentation:
Cost:
    logabsdet = O(D^3)
where:
    D = num of features

A general linear transform that uses an unconstrained weight matrix.

This transform explicitly computes the log absolute determinant in the forward direction
and uses a linear solver in the inverse direction.

Both forward and inverse directions have a cost of O(D^3), where D is the dimension
of the input.

An invertible 1x1 convolution with a fixed permutation, as introduced in the Glow paper.

classes.name: nflows.transforms.orthogonal.HouseholderSequence

methods.name: nflows.transforms.orthogonal.HouseholderSequence.__init__
documentation:
Constructor.

Args:
    features: int, dimensionality of the input.
    num_transforms: int, number of Householder transforms to use.

Raises:
    TypeError: if arguments are not the right type.

methods.name: nflows.transforms.orthogonal.HouseholderSequence._apply_transforms
documentation:
Apply the sequence of transforms parameterized by given q_vectors to inputs.

Costs O(KDN), where:
- K is number of transforms
- D is dimensionality of inputs
- N is number of inputs

Args:
    inputs: Tensor of shape [N, D]
    q_vectors: Tensor of shape [K, D]

Returns:
    A tuple of:
    - A Tensor of shape [N, D], the outputs.
    - A Tensor of shape [N], the log absolute determinants of the total transform.

methods.name: nflows.transforms.orthogonal.HouseholderSequence.matrix
documentation:
Returns the orthogonal matrix that is equivalent to the total transform.

Costs O(KD^2), where:
- K is number of transforms
- D is dimensionality of inputs

Returns:
    A Tensor of shape [D, D].

A sequence of Householder transforms.

This class can be used as a way of parameterizing an orthogonal matrix.

modules.name: nflows.transforms.normalization
documentation: Implementation of normalization-based transforms.

classes.name: nflows.transforms.normalization.BatchNorm
documentation:
Transform that performs batch normalization.

Limitations:
    * It works only for 1-dim inputs.
    * Inverse is not available in training mode, only in eval mode.
class BatchNorm(Transform):
"""Transform that performs batch normalization.
Limitations:
* It works only for 1-dim inputs.
* Inverse is not available in training mode, only in eval mode.
def __init__(self, features, eps=1e-5, momentum=0.1, affine=True):
if not check.is_positive_int(features):
raise TypeError('Number of features must be a positive integer.')
super().__init__()
self.batch_norm = nets.BatchNorm1d(
num_features=features,
eps=eps,
momentum=momentum,
affine=affine,
track_running_stats=True,  # We mustn't use batch statistics in eval mode.
def forward(self, inputs):
if inputs.dim() != 2:
raise ValueError('Expected 2-dim inputs, got inputs of shape: {}'.format(inputs.shape))
outputs = self.batch_norm(inputs)
if self.training:
var = torch.var(inputs, dim=0, unbiased=False)
else:
var = self.batch_norm.running_var
logabsdet = -0.5 * torch.log(var + self.batch_norm.eps)
if self.batch_norm.affine:
logabsdet += torch.log(self.batch_norm.weight)
logabsdet = torch.sum(logabsdet)
logabsdet = logabsdet * torch.ones(inputs.shape[0])
return outputs, logabsdet
def inverse(self, inputs):
if self.training:
raise InverseNotAvailable(
'Batch norm inverse is only available in eval mode, not in training mode.')
if inputs.dim() != 2:
raise ValueError('Expected 2-dim inputs, got inputs of shape: {}'.format(inputs.shape))
outputs = inputs.clone()
if self.batch_norm.affine:
outputs -= self.batch_norm.bias
outputs /= self.batch_norm.weight
outputs *= torch.sqrt(self.batch_norm.running_var + self.batch_norm.eps)
outputs += self.batch_norm.running_mean
logabsdet = 0.5 * torch.log(self.batch_norm.running_var + self.batch_norm.eps)
if self.batch_norm.affine:
logabsdet -= torch.log(self.batch_norm.weight)
logabsdet = torch.sum(logabsdet)
logabsdet = logabsdet * torch.ones(inputs.shape[0])
return outputs, logabsdet

methods.name: nflows.transforms.normalization.ActNorm.__init__
documentation:
Transform that performs activation normalization. Works for 2D and 4D inputs. For 4D
inputs (images) normalization is performed per-channel, assuming BxCxHxW input shape.

Reference:
> D. Kingma et. al., Glow: Generative flow with invertible 1x1 convolutions, NeurIPS 2018.

methods.name: nflows.transforms.normalization.ActNorm._initialize
documentation:
Data-dependent initialization, s.t. post-actnorm activations have zero mean and unit
variance.

modules.name: nflows.nn.nets.mlp
documentation: Implementations multi-layer perceptrons.

classes.name: nflows.nn.nets.mlp.MLP

methods.name: nflows.nn.nets.mlp.MLP.__init__
documentation:
Args:
    in_shape: tuple, list or torch.Size, the shape of the input.
    out_shape: tuple, list or torch.Size, the shape of the output.
    hidden_sizes: iterable of ints, the hidden-layer sizes.
    activation: callable, the activation function.
    activate_output: bool, whether to apply the activation to the output.
A standard multi-layer perceptron.

classes.name: nflows.nn.nets.resnet.ResidualBlock
documentation: A general-purpose residual block. Works only with 1-dim inputs.

classes.name: nflows.nn.nets.resnet.ResidualNet
documentation: A general-purpose residual network. Works only with 1-dim inputs.

documentation: Tests for Real NVP.

modules.name: nflows.utils.torchutils
documentation: Various PyTorch utility functions.

functions.name: nflows.utils.torchutils.sum_except_batch
documentation: Sums all elements of `x` except for the first `num_batch_dims` dimensions.

functions.name: nflows.utils.torchutils.split_leading_dim
documentation: Reshapes the leading dim of `x` to have the given shape.

functions.name: nflows.utils.torchutils.merge_leading_dims
documentation: Reshapes the tensor `x` such that the first `num_dims` dimensions are merged to one.

functions.name: nflows.utils.torchutils.repeat_rows
documentation: Each row of tensor `x` is repeated `num_reps` times along leading dimension.

functions.name: nflows.utils.torchutils.logabsdet
documentation: Returns the log absolute determinant of square matrix x.

functions.name: nflows.utils.torchutils.random_orthogonal
documentation: Returns a random orthogonal matrix as a 2-dim tensor of shape [size, size].

functions.name: nflows.utils.torchutils.get_num_parameters
documentation:
Returns the number of trainable parameters in a model of type nets.Module
\:param model: nets.Module containing trainable parameters
\:return: number of trainable parameters in model

functions.name: nflows.utils.torchutils.create_alternating_binary_mask
documentation:
Creates a binary mask of a given dimension which alternates its masking.

```
param features

Dimension of mask.

param even

If True, even values are assigned 1s, odd 0s. If False, vice versa.

return

Alternating binary mask of type torch.Tensor.
```

functions.name: nflows.utils.torchutils.create_mid_split_binary_mask
documentation:
Creates a binary mask of a given dimension which splits its masking at the midpoint.

```
param features

Dimension of mask.

return

Binary mask split at midpoint of type torch.Tensor
```

functions.name: nflows.utils.torchutils.create_random_binary_mask
documentation:
Creates a random binary mask of a given dimension with half of its entries
randomly set to 1s.

```
param features

Dimension of mask.

return

Binary mask with half of its entries set to 1s, of type torch.Tensor.
```

functions.name: nflows.utils.torchutils.cbrt
documentation: Cube root. Equivalent to torch.pow(x, 1/3), but numerically stable.

functions.name: nflows.utils.torchutils.get_temperature
documentation:
For a dataset with max value 'max_value', returns the temperature such that

> sigmoid(temperature * max_value) = bound.

If temperature is greater than 1, returns 1.

```
param max_value



param bound



return

```

modules.name: nflows.transforms.nonlinearities
documentation: Implementations of invertible non-linearities.

classes.name: nflows.transforms.nonlinearities.LogTanh
documentation:
Tanh with unbounded output. 

Constructed by selecting a cut_point, and replacing values to the right of cut_point
with alpha * log(beta * x), and to the left of -cut_point with -alpha * log(-beta *
x). alpha and beta are set to match the value and the first derivative of tanh at
cut_point.

documentation: A linear module with a masked weight matrix.

classes.name: nflows.nn.nde.made.MaskedFeedforwardBlock
documentation:
A feedforward block based on a masked linear module.

NOTE: In this implementation, the number of output features is taken to be equal to
the number of input features.

classes.name: nflows.nn.nde.made.MaskedResidualBlock
documentation: A residual block containing masked linear modules.

classes.name: nflows.nn.nde.made.MADE
documentation:
Implementation of MADE.

It can use either feedforward blocks or residual blocks (default is residual).
Optionally, it can use batch norm or dropout within blocks (default is no).
